fields.summary: Developer cannot see SonarQube project after being added to the team
fields.description: A new developer Rohan Gupta joined our team last week and needs access to our SonarQube project inventory-service. I added him to the project via Administration > Project Settings > Permissions and gave him Browse permission. However when Rohan logs in he cannot see the inventory-service project in his dashboard at all. He can see other projects he was previously a member of from his previous team. Our SonarQube instance uses LDAP authentication.

content.text: I double checked and Rohan's account is showing as active in SonarQube. His username in SonarQube is rohan.gupta and I confirmed it matches his LDAP login. I gave him Browse permission on the project but he still cannot see it after logging out and back in.

Ananya, the issue is likely that the inventory-service project has private visibility. When a project is set to private, Browse permission at the project level is required AND the user must not be blocked by a global permission template that overrides project-level settings. Can you check under Administration > Projects > Management and look at what Permission Template is applied to inventory-service? There may be a template that restricts visibility to a specific group.

Found it. The inventory-service had the finance-team-only permission template applied which restricts Browse to the finance-ldap-group. Rohan is in the engineering group. I added Rohan directly to the project-level permission overriding the template. He can now see the project. Should we change the template or keep the direct permission?
Keep the direct permission for now and raise a separate ticket to review the permission template assignment. The direct permission is fine as a fix. Closing this ticket.
